generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance GantryCartMotorController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controller system for horizontal (cart) movement of the lab-scale gantry crane, interfacing with the motor via deterministic timing protocol"
]

instance GantryHoistMotorController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controller system for vertical (hoist) movement of the lab-scale gantry crane, implementing closed-loop PID control for precision positioning"
]

instance ElectromagneticPuckPositioner : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electromagnetic actuator for precise puck positioning within the crane's workspace, enabling load handling and positioning control"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance SwingAngleEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Rotational position encoder measuring the crane arm's swing angle with high precision for kinematic modeling"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SwingAngleDataStream
]

instance Anemometer : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Environmental sensor measuring wind speed for contextual operational data, though primarily used for environmental monitoring rather than crane operation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalDataStream
]

instance PositionEncoderCart : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder tracking the horizontal (cart) movement position of the gantry crane's base structure"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionEncoderCartDataStream
]

instance PositionEncoderHoist : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder tracking the vertical (hoist) movement position of the crane's lifting mechanism"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionEncoderHoistDataStream
]

instance LoadCell : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Force sensor measuring the crane's load capacity and distribution during material handling operations"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LoadDataStream
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance SwingAngleDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SwingAngleEncoder
]

instance PositionEncoderCartDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PositionEncoderCart
]

instance PositionEncoderHoistDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PositionEncoderHoist
]

instance LoadDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LoadCell
]

instance EnvironmentalDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom Anemometer
]

instance OperationalLogStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GantryCartMotorController, GantryHoistMotorController
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance KinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D CAD-based kinematic model of the lab-scale gantry crane structure (created in Autodesk Fusion360) with derived motion equations for trajectory generation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonKinematicLibrary
]

instance TrajectoryDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series database (TimescaleDB) storing historical operational data including position logs, velocity metrics, and power consumption profiles for offline analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperationalLogStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HistoricalDataAnalysisService
]

instance RealTimeTrajectory : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Dynamic trajectory data generated by the Trajectory Generator Service for real-time path planning and execution optimization"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EncoderDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ContinuousValidationService
]

instance SimulationResults : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation outputs from the Simulation Service comparing predicted vs actual crane performance metrics"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EncoderDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ContinuousValidationService
]

instance OperationalMetrics : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Performance metrics including trajectory accuracy, motion error thresholds, and control loop efficiency derived from real-time sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EncoderDataStream, LoadDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo OptimalControlService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance TimescaleDB : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database management system for storing and querying historical operational data with sub-second granularity"
    DTDFVocab:enables HistoricalDataAnalysisService, ContinuousValidationService
]

instance MQTTBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Mosquitto MQTT broker facilitating low-latency bidirectional communication between physical sensors and digital twin components"
    DTDFVocab:enables PhysicalCraneInterface
]

instance GrafanaDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Interactive visualization platform providing real-time and historical data dashboards for crane operation monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationService
]

instance Fusion360Visualization : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Autodesk Fusion360 visualization tools enabling 3D overlay of physical and digital states for trajectory tracking and model validation"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationService
]

instance CustomPLCInterface : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom-built deterministic control interface module interfacing with the crane's controller system for precise timing requirements"
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimalControlService
]

instance PythonKinematicLibrary : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom Python library implementing kinematic equations and motion planning algorithms for trajectory generation"
    DTDFVocab:enables TrajectoryGeneratorService
]

instance ContinuousValidationService : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time validation service comparing physical sensor data with digital twin kinematic model to detect discrepancies and ensure trajectory accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:enables ContinuousValidationService
]

// C6: Services
instance TrajectoryGeneratorService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service implementing trajectory generation algorithms using kinematic models and real-time sensor data for optimal path planning"
    DTDFVocab:provides TrajectoryOptimizationInsight
]

instance OptimalControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop control system implementing adaptive PID controllers to minimize motion errors and optimize trajectory execution with real-time feedback"
    DTDFVocab:provides TrajectoryOptimizationInsight, MotionErrorReductionAction
]

instance HistoricalDataAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Data processing service analyzing historical operational logs for trend analysis, fault detection, and system efficiency benchmarking"
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalTrendInsight, FaultPatternAnalysisAction
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization service providing synchronized 3D/2D views of physical crane state and digital twin model parameters"
    DTDFVocab:provides StateVisualizationInsight, PerformanceBenchmarkInsight
]

instance ContinuousValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time validation service comparing physical sensor data with digital twin kinematic model to detect discrepancies and ensure trajectory accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:provides TrajectoryAccuracyInsight, ModelValidationAlertAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance TrajectoryOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time insight providing optimal movement trajectories with minimal motion errors based on adaptive control algorithms"
]

instance MotionErrorReductionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic action dynamically adjusting actuator commands to reduce motion oscillations and improve trajectory tracking precision"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance OperationalTrendInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Historical insight analyzing long-term operational patterns including position consistency, power consumption trends, and maintenance requirements"
]

instance FaultPatternAnalysisAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action identifying recurring operational faults through historical data analysis, enabling predictive maintenance scheduling"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance StateVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of physical crane state (joint angles, path trajectories) overlaid with digital twin model parameters for continuous validation"
]

instance PerformanceBenchmarkInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comparative insight showing current performance metrics against historical benchmarks and optimal trajectory standards"
]

instance TrajectoryAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time validation insight quantifying trajectory accuracy by comparing physical sensor data with digital twin predictions"
]

instance ModelValidationAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Alert action triggered when validation thresholds are exceeded, prompting manual intervention for model refinement or sensor calibration"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C12: Physical Crane Interface
instance PhysicalCraneInterface : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Python-based interface container connecting the lab-scale gantry crane's controllers to the digital twin system via MQTT protocol"
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment LabEnvironment
]

instance LabEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains GantryCartMotorController, GantryHoistMotorController, SwingAngleEncoder,
              PositionEncoderCart, PositionEncoderHoist, LoadCell, Anemometer,
              KinematicModel, TrajectoryDatabase, RealTimeTrajectory, SimulationResults,
              OperationalMetrics, TimescaleDB, MQTTBroker, GrafanaDashboard,
              Fusion360Visualization, CustomPLCInterface, PythonKinematicLibrary,
              ContinuousValidationService, PhysicalCraneInterface
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
  The **virtual_to_physical_interaction** is implemented via the **Control Application**, which receives trajectory commands from the **Trajectory Generator Service** (a kinematics-based service generating optimal movement paths with zero residual swinging motion). The Control Application translates these commands into control signals sent through the **Physical Crane Interface** to the actual crane hardware.

  Command types include trajectory waypoints and motion parameters derived from the kinematics model. The **Human Machine Interface (HMI)** enables operator interaction, allowing manual overrides or adjustments to trajectories. No explicit message formats (e.g., JSON) or authentication mechanisms are described, but the **MQTT Broker** is mentioned for signaling between services (implying control messages may use it indirectly). Feedback systems are implied by the **Continuous Validation Service**, which monitors discrepancies between simulated and real trajectories, though conflict resolution or command execution confirmation are not detailed.

  The Control Application validates commands before execution, ensuring alignment with the crane’s operational constraints (e.g., kinematic limits). No additional protocols (e.g., HTTP/REST) or detailed feedback loops are specified.
  "
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "
The **GreenhouseDT** system employs a **low-cost, pre-recorded data-driven approach** with virtual machines hosting InfluxDB and ActiveMQ, suggesting asynchronous or batch-based synchronization rather than explicit real-time requirements. The use of pre-recorded data implies offline or semi-real-time processing without specified latency or granularity constraints.

The **Air Quality Management** exemplar leverages a **Raspberry-Pi with sensor inputs** and AutomationML for model-driven digital twin engineering, but no specific synchronization frequencies (e.g., millisecond-to-second granularity) or latency bounds (e.g., ≤100ms) are documented. The reliance on Microsoft Azure tools for runtime visualization/simulation implies cloud-based processing, but temporal specifics (e.g., polling intervals) are not provided.

For the **lab-scale gantry crane**, no details regarding synchronization frequencies, latency requirements, or temporal granularity are mentioned in the provided documents.
"
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
The **GreenhouseDT** exemplifies a **self-adaptive digital twin constellation** where both the physical greenhouse and its digital twin exhibit **behavioral adaptation** via **InfluxDB + ActiveMQ** integration. The system lacks a formal orchestration layer but relies on **ad-hoc containerization (VMs)** for deployment flexibility. The **Air Quality Management** employs an **AutomationML-based digital twin**, integrated with **Microsoft Azure tools** for runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction. This constellation includes:
- **Component Integration**: Raspberry Pi (edge) ↔ AutomationML model ↔ Azure cloud services (for prediction).
- **Resource Management**: Azure likely handles scalability for prediction workloads, while AutomationML ensures interoperability.
- **Coordination**: Real-time data streams (via sensors) are processed in parallel for visualization/simulation, with no explicit coordination pattern described.

The **lab-scale gantry crane** uses the **C4 Model** for architecture visualization but does not detail orchestration or coordination mechanisms. The **discussion** highlights that the **ARCHES-PiCar-X** uses **containerization** for modularity but lacks a commercial framework, implying **ad-hoc orchestration**.

**Not in Document** for explicit orchestration patterns or coordination mechanisms in the lab-scale gantry crane.
"
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
  **Development Stages:**
  - **GreenhouseDT:** Focuses on self-adaptive digital twins for agricultural systems, emphasizing real-time behavioral adaptation of both the physical greenhouse and its digital twin during operation.
  - **Air Quality Management:** Centers on model-driven digital twins for construction engineering, particularly for indoor air quality management and virus prevention. Applications include runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction, suggesting iterative development phases tied to operational needs.

  **Representation Types:**
  - **GreenhouseDT:** Uses pre-recorded data in virtual machines and relies on databases (InfluxDB) and message brokers (ActiveMQ) for real-time data synchronization.
  - **Air Quality Management:** Leverages AutomationML for digital twin representation and Microsoft Azure tools for pipeline implementation, indicating structured data modeling for interoperability.

  **Transition Management:**
  - Both exemplars appear to adopt an **ad-hoc approach** without explicit lifecycle governance frameworks. The transition between physical and digital states is managed via real-time sensor data (Raspberry Pi) and containerized environments (e.g., virtual machines, Docker-like setups).

  **Lifecycle Governance:**
  - **Not explicitly detailed** in the documents. The examples emphasize modular, domain-specific adaptations rather than standardized lifecycle governance.
  "
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The **GreenhouseDT** and **Air Quality Management** exemplars do not detail explicit validation methods beyond behavioral adaptation or model-driven engineering frameworks.

The **Lab Scale Gantry Crane** employs:
- **Continuous Validation Service**: Continuously compares **executed trajectory measurements** with **simulated trajectories** using distance metrics (e.g., Euclidean error) against an **acceptable threshold**.
No mention of fidelity requirements (e.g., 95% accuracy), uncertainty quantification, or error correction mechanisms (e.g., PID tuning) is provided.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
  The **digital twin’s technical connection** relies on a **containerized network infrastructure** deployed on a single personal computer. The **MQTT Broker** serves as the primary communication protocol for inter-service signaling between the **Trajectory Generator Service**, **Simulation Service**, **Continuous Validation Service**, and **Dashboard**. The topology is centralized, with all services hosted locally on the same machine.

  No explicit bandwidth or latency requirements are mentioned, but the system’s real-time dependencies imply minimal latency constraints (e.g., sub-second response times for trajectory validation). Reliability is ensured by the containerized deployment (e.g., Docker) for the **Database**, **MQTT Broker**, and **Dashboard**, though redundancy mechanisms are not detailed. Security protocols are not explicitly described, but the system’s local deployment implies minimal exposure to external threats.

  The **TimescaleDB database** acts as a centralized store for all logged data, with no mention of distributed or sharded architectures. The network topology is implicit: all services communicate via the MQTT broker, with no additional protocols (e.g., HTTP/REST) or peer-to-peer architectures.
  "
]

    base:desc "

The **Control Application** and **Physical Crane Interface** run as native Python processes.

  Computational requirements are not specified, but the system’s real-time operations imply moderate CPU and memory usage. Storage requirements are tied to the **TimescaleDB database**, which stores historical and real-time crane state data. ]

    base:desc "
The **GreenhouseDT** system integrates internally via **InfluxDB database** (for data storage) and **ActiveMQ message broker** (for real-time communication between components), but no details are provided regarding integration with external systems, enterprise platforms, or other digital twins.
